_comment: For each rubric criterion: a Doctor line that clearly SATISFIES it (met) plus a short Patient reply. A transcript INCLUDES the snippet when the criterion is labeled 'met' and OMITS it entirely when labeled 'missed'. Kept distinct to minimize one snippet accidentally satisfying another criterion.

intro_self.doctor: Hello, I'm Doctor Morgan, one of the doctors here in the clinic today.
intro_self.patient: Hi doctor, nice to meet you.

set_tone.doctor: Please make yourself comfortable — we've got plenty of time and it's just the two of us in here.

open_questions.doctor: So, in your own words, tell me what's been going on.
open_questions.patient: Well, I've been getting these headaches for a couple of weeks.

explore_complaint.doctor: When exactly did it start, how severe does it get, and is there anything that makes it better or worse?
explore_complaint.patient: About two weeks ago, worse in the afternoons, and screens make it worse.

avoid_interrupting.doctor: Please, go on and take your time — I'm listening, there's no rush.
avoid_interrupting.patient: Okay, well it also comes with a bit of tiredness.

what_else.doctor: Before we move on, is there anything else that's been worrying you that we haven't covered?

explore_perspective.doctor: What do you think might be causing it, and is there anything in particular you're worried it could be?
explore_perspective.patient: I was worried it might be something serious, like a tumour.

respond_emotion.doctor: I can see this has been really frightening for you, and that's completely understandable.
respond_emotion.patient: Yeah, it has been, thank you.

support_respect.doctor: You've done exactly the right thing coming in, and we'll work through this together.

explain_exam.doctor: I'm just going to check your blood pressure and look in your eyes now — let me know if anything feels uncomfortable.

plain_language.doctor: In simple terms, it's the muscles around your head tightening up — nothing dangerous.

accurate_info.doctor: Tension-type headaches like this are very common, usually harmless, and often linked to stress, screen time, or poor sleep.

shared_plan.doctor: There are a couple of options — we could try regular screen breaks and simple pain relief first, or start a preventer. What would you prefer?
shared_plan.patient: Let's try the screen breaks first.

check_understanding.doctor: Just so I know I've explained it clearly, could you tell me back what the plan is?
check_understanding.patient: Take breaks from screens, use paracetamol, and come back if it gets worse.

safety_net.doctor: If it suddenly gets much worse, you get any vision changes, weakness, or it wakes you from sleep, please seek help straight away or go to A&E.

invite_questions.doctor: Before we finish up, what questions do you have for me?
invite_questions.patient: No, I think you've covered everything, thank you.
